title: Trump considers restarting military strikes on Iran amid stalled nuclear talks
summary: President Trump received military briefings on strike options against Iranian infrastructure as nuclear negotiations stall. Iran demands payment to revert pre-war status quo while the US maintains Strait of Hormuz blockade. Oil prices surge past $125 per barrel.
contents:
# Trump Weighs Military Strike Options Against Iran

## Overview
President Trump received classified briefings from CENTCOM commander Admiral Brad Cooper and Joint Chiefs Chairman General Dan Caine on May 1, 2026, regarding potential military operations against Iran. The briefings outlined three strike packages as nuclear talks remain deadlocked.

## Strike Options Presented
- **Package A**: Short, powerful wave of strikes targeting Iranian infrastructure
- **Package B**: Operations to seize portions of the Strait of Hormuz for shipping restoration
- **Package C**: Special forces mission to secure Iran's enriched uranium stockpile

## Nuclear Dimension
The UN watchdog reports Iran possesses enough enriched uranium for up to 10 nuclear bombs if weaponized. Iran proposed decoupling its nuclear program from war-ending discussions and Strait reopening.

## Economic Impact
- Brent crude surged past $125 per barrel
- Iranian President Pezeshkian called the US port siege "intolerable"
- Iran mocked Trump over oil prices, predicting "$140 next"

## Political Context
Defense nominee Pete Hegseth faced congressional scrutiny over war costs and civilian deaths. He claimed the legal 60-day war limit paused during the current ceasefire. A senior Democrat accused Hegseth of misleading Trump on the war.

## Source Context
Multiple wire services and Pentagon correspondents report these developments. The briefing took place amid heightened tensions following weeks of stalled negotiations.

## Tags
